author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
dekhite dekhite, bhuliba vā kabe,
nija⋅sthūla⋅paricaya
nayane heriba, vrajapura⋅śobhā,
nitya cidānanda-maya

vṛṣabhānu-pure, janama loiba,
jāvaṭe vivāha ha’be
vrajagopī⋅bhāva, hoibe svabhāva,
āna⋅bhāva nā rahibe

nija siddhadeha, nija siddhanāma,
nija⋅rūpa⋅svavasana
rādhākṛpā⋅bale, labhiba vā kabe,
kṛṣṇaprema⋅prakaraṇa

jāmuna⋅salila, āharaṇe giyā,
bujhiba jugala⋅rasa
prema-mugdha ha’ye, pāgalinī⋅prāya,
gāiba rādhāra jaśa
translation_intro: From The Harmonist, April 1932. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
en_translation:
And by me unawares When shall I forget The memory of my gross self? Shall behold with my eyes The beauty of the realm of Braja Full of the eternal spiritual bliss?

In Brishabhanu's town When shall I have my birth, Shall be married in Yabata? The mood of the milkmaid of Braja Will be my nature, And there be no other disposition?

My own realised body, My own realised name, My own form, my own apparel, By the power of Radha's mercy, O when shall I gain? And initiation into the love of Krishna?

Going out to fetch The water of the Yamuna Shall understand the love of the Divine pair? And smitten by love, Like a distracted maiden, Shall sing the praise of Radha?
